note: Illustrative adverse-media corpus for local demo. Production deployments should plug in a licensed news feed (Refinitiv World-Check, Dow Jones Risk & Compliance, RDC, ComplyAdvantage) or a curated open-source signal (FATF case studies, FinCEN enforcement actions, SEC press releases).

tiers.label: Top-tier wire (Reuters, BBC, FT, WSJ, Bloomberg)
Trade press / specialist outlets / regulator press release

articles.headline: OFAC adds Trident Export Group to SDN list over trade-control evasion
articles.snippet: The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control on Tuesday designated Trident Export Group Limited and three affiliated shell entities, citing a multi-year scheme to re-route restricted dual-use goods through Cypriot and Emirati intermediaries.

articles.headline: Trident Exports faces second customs probe in six months
articles.snippet: Indian Customs has opened a second probe into Trident Exports' Mumbai operations after intercepting a consignment of mis-declared chemicals bound for a Russian end-user, sources familiar with the matter said.

articles.headline: Crescent Maritime vessels caught in Strait of Hormuz STS transfers
articles.snippet: Two tankers operated by Crescent Maritime and a Cyprus-flag vessel performed at least four ship-to-ship transfers in the Strait of Hormuz over the past quarter, satellite imagery analysed by TankerTrackers shows.

articles.headline: Belarusian businessman Aliekseii Volkov-Baranov named in EU sanctions package
articles.snippet: Brussels added Belarusian procurement intermediary Aliekseii Volkov-Baranov to its restrictive-measures list, alleging he brokered electronic components for a sanctioned defence-electronics conglomerate.

articles.headline: Pyongyang Horizon front company tied to DPRK weapons procurement
articles.snippet: A UN Panel of Experts report names Pyongyang New Horizon Cooperative as a critical front in the procurement of foreign-sourced ballistic-missile components, breaching UNSC Resolution 1718.

articles.headline: Golden Oryx Trading FZE blocked by UAE authorities pending audit
articles.snippet: UAE's Executive Office for Anti-Money Laundering froze the accounts of free-zone trader Golden Oryx Trading FZE this week after a joint investigation with OFAC alleged end-user diversion of dual-use chemicals.

articles.headline: Aurelia Shell Limited charged in cross-border layering scheme
articles.snippet: Mauritian prosecutors filed charges against Aurelia Shell Limited and four directors, alleging a layering operation that moved more than $80 million through nominee accounts between 2022 and 2025.

articles.headline: Argentum Horizon GmbH executives indicted in Frankfurt over fictitious invoicing
articles.snippet: BaFin referred Argentum Horizon GmbH to Frankfurt prosecutors after a multi-year probe uncovered roughly €40 million in fictitious commodity invoices, prosecutors confirmed on Wednesday.

articles.headline: Northern Steel Co named in DOJ trade-based laundering complaint
articles.snippet: The U.S. Department of Justice's unsealed complaint identifies Northern Steel Co as a counterparty in a trade-based money-laundering scheme that allegedly mis-priced steel shipments to disguise proceeds of Russian sanctions evasion.

articles.headline: Devraj Industries on the receiving end of FATF mutual-evaluation case study
articles.snippet: FATF's latest mutual-evaluation working paper cites Devraj Industries as a case study of an Indian SME used as a pass-through for proceeds of an alleged customs-misdeclaration ring.

articles.headline: Lakshmi Holdings Pvt Ltd settles SEBI insider-trading probe for ₹120 crore
articles.snippet: Lakshmi Holdings Pvt Ltd has agreed to a ₹120 crore settlement with India's market regulator SEBI over alleged insider trading in three listed entities, the regulator said in a press release on Friday.

articles.headline: Vikram Enterprises directors arrested on tax-fraud charges
articles.snippet: Two directors of Vikram Enterprises were arrested by India's Directorate General of GST Intelligence on charges of operating fake input-credit invoices worth an estimated ₹85 crore.

articles.headline: Rohit Mehta Trading flagged by RBI for layered foreign-remittance pattern
articles.snippet: An RBI inspection report referenced by IndianExpress identified Rohit Mehta Trading among a small set of Mumbai trading houses whose outward remittance pattern triggered an enhanced-due-diligence review.

articles.headline: Sundar Logistics under investigation for under-invoicing
articles.snippet: DRI is examining Sundar Logistics' shipping records on suspicion of under-invoicing electronics imports to evade customs duty, two officials briefed on the matter said.

articles.headline: Crescent Maritime cleared in 2021 sanctions probe — re-emergence remains a concern
articles.snippet: While Crescent Maritime was formally cleared by OFAC of allegations stemming from a 2021 STS-transfer probe, analysts at the Atlantic Council warned that the operator's pattern of flag-hopping deserves continued monitoring.

articles.headline: Federal jury convicts Eastvale Capital founders of $400m Ponzi scheme
articles.snippet: A Manhattan federal jury convicted the two co-founders of Eastvale Capital LLC of operating a $400 million Ponzi scheme that recruited investors through Indian-diaspora community networks.

articles.headline: FINRA suspends Helios Securities over wash-trading allegations
articles.snippet: FINRA suspended Helios Securities Inc and barred two principals for operating wash-trade schemes on small-cap names traded on the OTC markets, the regulator said in a Wednesday notice.

articles.headline: OFAC settles with Nautilus Marine over five years of sanctions screening failures
articles.snippet: Nautilus Marine Holdings agreed to a $14 million settlement with OFAC over what regulators described as systemic failures in screening counterparties in its bunker-fuel business.

articles.headline: PEP exposure flagged in Cyprus golden-passport file naming Petrov Holdings
articles.snippet: Leaked internal Cypriot files reviewed by OCCRP identify Petrov Holdings AG as a vehicle used by a Russian PEP to obtain Cypriot citizenship-by-investment status in 2017.

articles.headline: U.S. DOJ charges Banyan Pharma executives in opioid distribution fraud
articles.snippet: Three executives of Banyan Pharmaceuticals were charged in the Southern District of New York with operating a kickback scheme that flooded retail pharmacies with opioids in violation of the Controlled Substances Act.

articles.headline: Singapore MAS revokes Apex Trust's capital-markets licence over AML failures
articles.snippet: MAS revoked the capital-markets licence of Apex Trust Singapore citing systemic deficiencies in its anti-money-laundering controls, including failure to file suspicious transaction reports on at least 22 cases.

articles.headline: Suspected APT-linked breach at Meridian Cloud exposed 6m customer records
articles.snippet: A breach at Meridian Cloud, attributed by Mandiant to an unnamed APT, exposed approximately six million customer records, including financial-services credentials, the company disclosed in an 8-K filing.

articles.headline: Falcon Aviation Services bankruptcy filing leaves $90m unsecured creditor gap
articles.snippet: Falcon Aviation Services Pvt Ltd filed for liquidation under India's IBC framework, listing roughly ₹750 crore in liabilities and a $90 million unsecured creditor gap, regulatory filings show.

articles.headline: Court reinstates wire-fraud charges against directors of Verdant Realty
articles.snippet: An appellate court reinstated wire-fraud charges against two directors of Verdant Realty after lower-court rulings had dismissed them, allowing the criminal case to proceed.

articles.headline: U.S. SEC sues Cobalt Markets over rigged municipal-bond auctions
articles.snippet: The Securities and Exchange Commission filed a civil enforcement action against Cobalt Markets, alleging the firm and three traders rigged at least 11 municipal-bond auctions between 2020 and 2024.

articles.headline: OFAC adds two Crimea-based Crescent Logistics affiliates to SDN list
articles.snippet: OFAC designated two Crimea-based affiliates of Crescent Logistics LLC, citing their role in shipping sanctioned petroleum products and Russian-origin grain.

articles.headline: Spanish AEPD fines Trident Exports €2.4m for cross-border data violations
articles.snippet: Spain's Agencia Española de Protección de Datos fined Trident Exports €2.4 million for transferring personal data of EU customers to a sanctioned jurisdiction without an approved adequacy mechanism.

articles.headline: Magnolia Capital under SFO probe over alleged £200m carbon-credit fraud
articles.snippet: The UK Serious Fraud Office opened a probe into Magnolia Capital and three associated trading entities over an alleged £200m fraud involving voluntary carbon credits.

articles.headline: Argentum Horizon GmbH executives indicted on bribery charges in Frankfurt
articles.snippet: Frankfurt prosecutors indicted three executives of Argentum Horizon GmbH on bribery charges, alleging payments of more than €4 million to Turkish customs officials.

articles.headline: FATF mentions Apex Trust pattern in its annual mutual-evaluation digest
articles.snippet: The FATF's annual digest of mutual-evaluation findings cited the Apex Trust Singapore case as illustrative of weak control environments in mid-sized fiduciary firms.

articles.headline: OCCRP reveals Petrov Holdings beneficial-ownership chain spanning seven jurisdictions
articles.snippet: OCCRP's deep-dive on Petrov Holdings AG traces a beneficial-ownership chain spanning Cyprus, the BVI, Liechtenstein, the UAE, Singapore, the Seychelles, and Mauritius, naming the ultimate beneficial owner as a designated Russian PEP.

articles.headline: Falcon Aviation re-incorporated under new name, raising phoenix-company concerns
articles.snippet: A successor entity to liquidated Falcon Aviation Services has been incorporated in the same Mumbai building under the name Falcon Air & Logistics, raising phoenix-company concerns among creditors.

articles.headline: Eastvale Capital subject to investor class-action over feeder-fund disclosures
articles.snippet: Investors filed a securities-class action against Eastvale Capital LLC in the Southern District of New York, alleging inadequate disclosures about leverage and counterparty exposure in its flagship feeder fund.

articles.headline: BSA enforcement action: Apex Trust pays $9m for SAR-filing lapses
articles.snippet: FinCEN imposed a $9 million civil money penalty on Apex Trust Singapore's U.S. branch for failing to file timely SARs on a high-volume corridor of Asia-Pacific wire transfers.

articles.headline: Meridian Cloud customers report follow-on phishing campaign tied to breach data
articles.snippet: Customers of Meridian Cloud reported a follow-on phishing campaign using personal details exposed in the firm's March breach, with at least 1,800 fraudulent ACH transfers reported by mid-May.

articles.headline: Devraj Industries cleared in 2022 IT-Act case, returns to active operations
articles.snippet: Devraj Industries was cleared by an Income Tax Appellate Tribunal in a 2022 case involving disputed deductions, allowing the company to resume normal operations across its three Maharashtra units.

articles.headline: Verdant Realty board chair resigns after fresh wire-fraud allegations
articles.snippet: Verdant Realty's board chair stepped down on Friday following the appellate-court reinstatement of wire-fraud charges against two directors, the company said in a regulatory filing.

articles.headline: Cypriot regulator examines Petrov Holdings golden-passport ties
articles.snippet: Cyprus' financial-services regulator opened a parallel examination into the golden-passport ties of Petrov Holdings AG, mirroring a broader EU push to revoke citizenship granted under the now-defunct CIP.

articles.headline: Cobalt Markets settles with SEC, pays $32m, two principals barred
articles.snippet: Cobalt Markets reached a $32 million settlement with the SEC over the municipal-bond auction probe, with two principals barred from the securities industry for life.

articles.headline: FATF working group highlights Aurelia Shell case as benchmark for layering detection
articles.snippet: FATF's evaluators highlighted the Aurelia Shell Limited case as a benchmark for layering-detection methodologies, citing its multi-hop nominee structure spanning Mauritius, the UAE, and Singapore.
